name: re-asserting a relationship after negation must not leave a negated zombie
description: Adversarial assertion-transition probe, now fixed. Note 1 negates the worksFor edge ('Bjorn no longer works at Acme', assertion=negated). Note 2 re-asserts it ('Bjorn is back at Acme', assertion=asserted) — same functional predicate, same object entity Acme. Previously supersession.values_equal keyed on object_entity_id only for a pure edge, so the asserted candidate was 'equal' to the still-active negated row and decide returned refresh_id: the pipeline refreshed the NEGATED row in place and never updated its assertion, leaving a negated zombie that read as 'not working there' when the truth was the opposite. SPEC-CORRECT expectation encoded: an active ASSERTED worksFor to Acme after re-assertion, and NO active negated one. Fixed: values_equal returns False on a flipped assertion, so the re-assertion supersedes the negated head and the new active row carries assertion=asserted.

steps.body: Bjorn no longer works at Acme — he left last month.

facts.statement: Bjorn no longer works at Acme.

steps.body: Bjorn is back at Acme again — rehired this week.
